Econometrics · Difference-in-Differences · Project #4

Minimum Wage & Low-Wage Employment — Interactive Results Dashboard

Causal effect of statutory state minimum-wage increases on Leisure & Hospitality employment, estimated by staggered difference-in-differences. State×quarter panel, 2010Q1–2025Q4, 51 jurisdictions, N = 3,264. All data from FRED. Hover any chart for exact values; toggle light/dark at top-right.

−2.1%
Effect of a state's first minimum-wage increase on L&H employment (Callaway–Sant'Anna ATT)
−0.0217 log pts · SE 0.0168 · 95% CI [−5.30%, +1.12%] · not statistically distinguishable from zero · robust to staggered-adoption correction

Key facts

Panel scope and treatment structure.

51
jurisdictions
50 states + DC
64
quarters
2010Q1–2025Q4
3,264
observations
balanced, 0 missing
307
increase events
31 treated states
20
never-treated
controls (incl. 5 no-statute)
441
ATT(g,t) cells
estimated by CSA

Data & pipeline

Five FRED series per jurisdiction; monthly series collapsed to quarterly means, the annual minimum wage forward-filled to quarters. 205 series in the map — 200 resolved, 5 dropped (not imputed).

RoleFRED seriesMeasuresFrequency
TreatmentSTTMINWG<XX>State minimum wage ($/hr)annual
Outcome<XX>LEIHLeisure & Hospitality employment (000s)monthly
Normalizer<XX>NATotal nonfarm employment (000s)monthly
Control<XX>URState unemployment rate (%)monthly
FlagFEDMINNFRWGFederal minimum wage ($7.25, flat)monthly

Unique series: 205. Cached: 191. Fetched: 9. Missing/discontinued: 5. Transient errors: 0. Panel: 3,264 rows = 51 × 64; gap quarters = 0; missing cells = 0.

StateSeries IDReason dropped (not imputed)
ALSTTMINWGALno state statute — remains a federal-floor control
LASTTMINWGLAno state statute — remains a federal-floor control
MSSTTMINWGMSno state statute — remains a federal-floor control
SCSTTMINWGSCno state statute — remains a federal-floor control
TNSTTMINWGTNno state statute — remains a federal-floor control

The 5 dropped series are the state-minimum-wage series for the no-statute states; their LEIH / NA / UR resolve normally, so they remain in the panel as never-treated federal-floor controls.

Treatment structure

An event = a state minimum-wage increase whose new level sits above the binding $7.25 federal floor. 307 events across 31 states, 8 staggered yearly cohorts. Median increase 5.4%. By 2025Q4 the treated-state mean effective minimum wage is $13.71 vs the $7.25 federal floor.

Every minimum-wage increase event (all 307)
x = event year, y = increase size; marker size/colour scale with the dollar increase. Hover for state and prev→new wage.
How to use: Each dot is one state minimum-wage increase. Higher up = a bigger percentage raise; bigger, darker dots = a larger dollar jump. Hover a dot to see the state, the year, and the old → new wage. Drag a box over part of the chart to zoom in, then click Reset view to zoom back out.
First-treatment cohort sizes
Number of states whose first qualifying increase falls in each cohort year. Largest cohort: 2011 (7 states).
How to use: Each bar counts how many states raised their minimum wage for the first time in that year. Hover a bar for the exact number of states. Click Reset view to undo any zoom.
First-treatment cohort# statesStates
2010Q16AK, CT, DC, IL, ME, NV
2011Q17AZ, CO, MT, OH, OR, VT, WA
2012Q11FL
2013Q12MO, RI
2014Q16CA, DE, MI, MN, NJ, NY
2015Q17AR, HI, MA, MD, NE, SD, WV
2020Q11NM
2021Q11VA

Never-treated controls (20): AL, GA, IA, ID, IN, KS, KY, LA, MS, NC, ND, NH, OK, PA, SC, TN, TX, UT, WI, WY. No-statute states (5, subset): AL, LA, MS, SC, TN.

Main results — estimator comparison

Outcome log(L&H employment); treatment = a state's first increase above the federal floor. Pooled-OLS SE is HC1; TWFE SE clustered by 51 states; CSA SE analytic/entity-clustered, pointwise CI.

Estimated % effect with 95% CI
Bars = point estimate; whiskers = 95% confidence interval. Callaway–Sant'Anna (blue) is the staggered-robust headline. Every CI crosses zero.
How to use: Each bar is one statistical method's estimated effect on jobs. The thin vertical line through the bar is the 95% confidence range — where the true effect most plausibly lies. If that line crosses the zero line, the effect is not statistically clear. Hover a bar for the exact figures.
EstimatorCoef (log pts)SE95% CI (log pts)% effect95% CI (%)pStaggered-robust
Pooled OLS (no FE)−0.00630.0343[−0.0735, +0.0608]−0.63%[−7.09, +6.27]%0.853no
TWFE DiD (state+period FE)−0.02680.0140[−0.0543, +0.0007]−2.64%[−5.29, +0.07]%0.057no
Callaway-Sant'Anna (2021)−0.02170.0168[−0.0545, +0.0112]−2.14%[−5.30, +1.12]%—yes
The staggered-adoption correction barely moves the headline. TWFE (−0.0268) and Callaway–Sant'Anna (−0.0217) differ by +0.0051 log pts — 19% of the TWFE magnitude. Moving from pooled OLS to TWFE shifts the estimate by −0.0204 log pts: the size of the confounding the design strips out.

TWFE event study & parallel-trends test

Relative-event-time leads/lags around each state's first increase; base period k = −1; endpoint bins at k ≤ −12 and k ≥ 16. State + period FE, SE clustered by state. Sample: 25 treated (with a pre-period) + 20 never-treated = 45 states, 2,880 obs.

TWFE dynamic ATT (leads & lags)
Points = coefficients (grey = pre-treatment leads, blue = post); shaded band = 95% CI; dashed red line marks treatment (k = −1 base).
How to use: Left-to-right is time around the wage hike, where 0 is the quarter the wage rose. Dots to the left of 0 are ‘before’, dots to the right are ‘after’; the shaded band is the 95% confidence range. Roughly flat dots before 0 are the sign the method can be trusted. Hover any dot for its value; drag to zoom, then Reset view.
Parallel-trends test (TWFE event study)FpVerdict
All 11 leads jointly = 030.160.001reject at 5%
Near window k ∈ [−4, −2] = 03.690.297fail to reject

4 of 11 lead coefficients individually significant at 5% (k = −12, −11, −10, −9) — concentrated in distant leads; near pre-window jointly flat. 6 of 17 post coefficients individually significant; post path negative and widening.

Full TWFE event-study coefficients (all 29 leads/lags)
ktypecoef (log pts)SE95% CIp% effect
<= -12lead+0.03640.0133[+0.0104, +0.0624]0.006+3.71%
-11lead+0.03100.0114[+0.0086, +0.0534]0.007+3.15%

Callaway–Sant'Anna (2021) — staggered-robust

Doubly-robust group-time ATT via the differences package. Cohort = quarter of a state's first increase; control = never-treated states. 31 ever-treated + 20 never-treated; 6 always-treated states dropped automatically. 441 ATT(g,t) cells estimated.

AggregationATT (log pts)SE95% CI% effect
Overall (group-size weighted)−0.02170.0168[−0.0545, +0.0112]−2.17%
Overall (event/post-avg)−0.01860.0193[−0.0564, +0.0191]−1.86%
Cohort-robust dynamic ATT
Points = ATT by event time (grey pre, blue post); band = 95% pointwise CI. Trough ≈ −1.1% near k = 4.
How to use: Same idea as the chart on the left, using the staggered-robust method. 0 marks the wage hike; dots before it are ‘before’ and after it are ‘after’, with the shaded 95% confidence band. Hover any dot for its exact value.
ATT by adoption cohort
Per-cohort overall ATT (blue = negative, gold = positive) with 95% CI. Negative for 2013–2015 & 2020; positive for 2012 & 2021.
How to use: Each bar is the estimated job effect for the group of states that first raised wages in that year. Blue = employment dipped, gold = employment rose; the thin line is the 95% confidence range. Hover a bar for exact values.
Cohort#StatesATT (log pts)SE95% CI% effectSig. 5%

2021Q11VA+0.02690.0078[+0.0117, +0.0421]+2.69%yes

Near-window |ATT| max (k ∈ [−4,−1]) = 0.0074 log pts. Post path deepens to a trough of −0.0109 log pts (−1.08%) at k = 4, then partially mean-reverts. No interior post coefficient individually significant at 5% (pointwise).

Descriptive evidence

Policy variation and raw trends (nothing causal). Treated and control L&H track closely pre-2014 (mean absolute index gap = 0.24 pts).

Effective minimum-wage paths
Treated-state mean effective minimum wage vs the flat $7.25 federal floor that binds controls. Clear policy variation to exploit.
How to use: Blue = the average minimum wage in states that raised it; dashed grey = the unchanged $7.25 federal floor that binds the comparison states. The widening gap between the lines is the policy change being studied. Hover a point for the dollar amount.
L&H employment index (2010Q1 = 100)
Treated vs never-treated group means, indexed to the panel start. The eyeball parallel-trends check.
How to use: Both lines start at 100 in 2010 so their growth is directly comparable. Blue = states that raised the minimum wage, gold = states that never did. The two lines moving together early on is the key assumption behind the whole analysis. Click a label in the legend to hide or show a line.

Outlier scan — largest quarter-on-quarter Δlog moves (60 exceed 4σ, σ = 0.0766)

The largest swings are the 2020Q2 COVID drop (and 2020Q3 rebound), not data errors — retained; period FE absorb them.

State (2020Q2)Δlog L&H
DC−0.920
NY−0.850
MA−0.799
HI−0.784
NJ−0.758
VT−0.754
RI−0.722
PA−0.709

Placebo & robustness

The design finds no effect where none should exist, and the estimate is stable to specification changes. Verdict: PASS (fake-date margins read as “no clear failure” rather than a pristine null).

High-wage-sector placebo — L&H vs PBSV
% effect for the main outcome (L&H) vs the placebo sector (Professional & Business Services), TWFE and Callaway–Sant'Anna. PBSV is near zero, as it should be.
How to use: A sanity check. The left pair of bars is the low-wage industry we actually study; the right pair is a high-wage industry the minimum wage should barely touch. We want the right pair sitting near zero — if it were large, the method would be suspect. Hover a bar for the exact percent.
Estimator (outcome = log PBSV)ATT (log pts)SE95% CI% effectp
PBSV TWFE−0.01680.0158[−0.0478, +0.0142]−1.66%0.289
PBSV Callaway–Sant'Anna−0.00210.0182[−0.0377, +0.0335]−0.21%—

Fake-event-date falsification (treatment shifted −8 quarters)

Test (outcome = log L&H, treatment shifted −8q)EstimateSE95% CIp
Fake DiD (single fake_treated_post)−0.01270.0078[−0.0279, +0.0024]0.100
Joint Wald: post fake-dummies = 0F = 13.84——0.086
Joint Wald: leads = 0F = 16.30——0.023

Treated states with a clean pre-fake window: 17; controls: 20. The fake DiD (p = 0.100) and the post-fake-dummy joint test (p = 0.086) do not reject; the distant pre-fake leads jointly reject (p = 0.023).

TWFE on restricted event windows
ATT re-estimated on |k| ≤ W. Stays in a narrow negative band; whiskers = 95% CI.
How to use: Re-runs the estimate using only data within 8, 12, or 16 quarters of each wage hike. If the bars barely move, the result isn't an artifact of one particular window. The thin line on each bar is the 95% confidence range. Hover for values.
CSA dynamic averaged over post-horizons
Average cohort-robust ATT over k ∈ [0, kmax] for growing horizons. Small and stable.
How to use: The average effect when we look 1, 2, 3, and 4 years after the hike (the x-axis lists the quarter ranges). A fairly flat line means the effect is stable over time. Hover a point for its value.

Source figures (static)

The original publication figures (matplotlib, 300 DPI), embedded for completeness. The interactive charts above are built from the same underlying data.

Show all 13 source figures

Caveats & limitations

Bottom line: no evidence of large minimum-wage disemployment in U.S. Leisure & Hospitality, 2010–2025; the data cannot rule out a small negative effect.

On/off treatment

Treatment is a binary indicator for a state's first increase, not the dollar size or subsequent raises. The estimate is an average post-adoption shift, not a per-dollar elasticity.

Statistically insignificant

Every credible 95% CI includes zero. The data are consistent with a modest negative effect, a true null, or a small positive effect; they rule out only large losses (lower bounds ≈ −5%).

Distant pre-trend

The TWFE lead test rejects parallel trends on far leads (k ≤ −9); the near pre-window and the CSA pre-period are approximately flat, but parallel trends are not cleanly satisfied across the full horizon.

Always-treated dropped

6 jurisdictions (AK, CT, DC, IL, ME, NV) have their first increase at the 2010Q1 panel start, contribute no clean pre-period, and are excluded from the event study and CSA.

Pointwise CSA CIs

CSA bands are analytic and pointwise; simultaneous (multiplier-bootstrap) bands would be modestly wider. Individual-cell significance should be read with that in mind.

Sector proxy & Q1 timing

L&H contains non-minimum-wage workers, attenuating any true effect toward zero. Events are dated to Q1 (annual series resolution); mid-year statutory changes cannot be timed more precisely.

Minimum Wage & Low-Wage Employment — Project #4 · Proteek Basu · Pre-Masters Economics Portfolio (Georgia Tech MS prep)
Methods: pooled OLS, two-way fixed effects, TWFE event study, Callaway–Sant'Anna (2021) group-time ATT. Data: FRED. Charts: Plotly (CDN). Generated by src/build_dashboard.py from results/.
